summary: A two-story residential building with a stepped gable roof. The structure features a rectangular footprint with a white concrete foundation/platform. The main walls are constructed from sandstone blocks with regular window openings on both floors. A brown wood or plank trim band separates the two stories. The roof is a prominent stepped gable design made of brick, with a small chimney visible at the peak.

shape.overall_form: rectangular box with stepped gable roof

elements: windows (6 per floor, arranged in 3x2 grid pattern)
rebuild_hints: Start with white concrete platform base, approximately 14x12 blocks
Build two-story walls using sandstone blocks with gray corner stones
Install 6 window openings per floor in 3x2 grid pattern, evenly spaced
Add brown wood/plank trim band horizontally between first and second floors
Construct stepped gable roof using brick, stepping inward as it rises
Place small chimney structure at the peak of the gable
Ensure bilateral symmetry along the front-back axis
Top of foundation platform should be 1 block above ground level
uncertainties: Exact window frame material (appears to be dark wood or dark glass frames)
Interior layout and room divisions not visible
Precise height of each floor (estimated at 4-5 blocks per story)
Whether corner blocks are stonebrick or another variant
Exact depth of the building (estimated from perspective)
Presence of doors or entrance points not clearly visible in provided views
